label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out step by step. So the question is whether following this set of instructions will bring me back to the starting point. The options are yes or no. Alright, let's break down the instructions one by one and track my position and direction. First, the starting point is where I am initially. Let's assume I'm facing north. Sometimes, these problems assume you start facing a specific direction, like north, unless told otherwise. So I'll go with that. 1. Turn right. If I was facing north, turning right would make me face east. So now I'm facing east. 2. Take 9 steps. Since I'm facing east, I'll move 9 steps to the east. Let's note that my position is now (9, 0) if we consider the starting point as (0, 0), with east as the positive x-axis and north as the positive y-axis. 3. Take 10 steps. Still facing east, so I take another 10 steps. Adding to my previous position: 9 + 10 = 19 steps east. Now I'm at (19, 0). 4. Turn left. Facing east, turning left would make me face north now. Because left from east is north. So now direction is north. 5. Take 10 steps. Moving north from (19, 0) by 10 steps. New position is (19, 10). 6. Turn right. Facing north, turning right would make me face east. Now direction is east again. 7. Turn around. Turning around means turning 180 degrees. So facing east, turning around makes me face west. So now direction is west. 8. Take 4 steps. Moving west from (19, 10) by 4 steps. Subtracting from x-coordinate: 19 - 4 = 15. New position is (15, 10). So after all instructions, I end up at (15, 10). The starting point was (0, 0). So unless there's a miscalculation here, I don't return to the starting point. Therefore, the answer should be No. Wait, but let me double-check my steps to make sure I didn't make an error. Starting at (0,0) facing north. 1. Turn right: facing east. 2. 9 steps east: (9, 0). 3. 10 steps east: 9 +10 = 19, so (19,0). 4. Turn left: facing north. Correct, left from east is north. 5. 10 steps north: (19,10). Correct. 6. Turn right: from north, right is east. Correct. 7. Turn around: from east, turn around to west. Correct. 8. Take 4 steps west: subtract 4 from x. 19-4=15. So (15,10). Yes. So ending at (15,10). Not back to starting point. Therefore, answer is No. Alternatively, maybe the initial direction isn't north? Let me try assuming a different starting direction, but usually, these problems assume you start facing north unless stated otherwise. But just to test, suppose I start facing south. Then turning right would face west. But that complicates the coordinate system. However, regardless of the initial direction, the relative movements should still be the same. Because turning right, then taking steps, then another turn, etc. So the net movement would be the same in terms of directions relative to each other.
